Tests the behavior of `isNaN` with `__proto__` in a try-catch block.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that tests the behavior of the `isNaN` function when it is called with `__proto__` as an argument. The script uses a `try-catch` block to handle any exceptions that might occur during the execution of `isNaN(__proto__)`. The purpose of this script is to ensure that calling `isNaN` with `__proto__` does not cause an unhandled exception, which could indicate a potential issue in the JavaScript environment's handling of this global property. The script does not perform any operations in the `catch` block, indicating that it is only interested in catching and suppressing any errors that might arise.
